summary: Comprehensive end-to-end testing of SEO Command Centre. 16/16 backend pytest cases pass against public preview URL. All 8 frontend pages load, sidebar nav works for all 8 routes, every data-testid in the spec is present and functional. Quick audit, site auditor, schema generator, location pages (Preston town render), meta optimiser, keyword density, all 50 SEO checklist tasks, task toggle persistence, dashboard streak/completion update, and competitor compare with emerald-400 highlight all verified working.

minor.issue: Code in server.py builds a `business` dict with @type=['LocalBusiness','Photograph'] then immediately overwrites it to ['LocalBusiness','ProfessionalService'] and discards it in favour of a separate `photographer` dict. Output is correct but the dead code is confusing/wasteful.

STOPWORDS literal contains a stray '".split' substring (looks like an accidental paste) inside the triple-quoted source string. The set still gets built via .split() so it works, but the token list is slightly polluted with the literal word 'split'.

action_items: (Optional polish) Clean up the dead @type assignments in /api/schema for clarity.
(Optional polish) Fix the STOPWORDS triple-quoted string so it doesn't include the literal '".split' fragment.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 976 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline; consider splitting into routers (audit.py, schema.py, locations.py, tasks.py, compare.py) and helpers/analyse.py for maintainability.

Audit results stored in Mongo include the full `checks` and `recommendations` arrays — fine for now but listing /api/audits with limit=25 returns the full payload of every audit. The dashboard endpoint projects only summary fields, but /api/audits should consider doing the same for list pagination.
Bad-URL handling on /api/audit returns 502 with detail from the underlying httpx exception — works, but consider wrapping with a friendlier user-facing message.
No async lock on tasks_completed upsert — fine for a single-user personal tool, but mention in deployment docs.
CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — a known FastAPI footgun. Personal tool so low risk, but worth flagging for the TrueNAS deployment.
test_credentials: N/A — app is auth-less by design (personal tool).
seed_data_creation: Test toggles task 'ssl' on then off, leaving DB in original state. One real audit of perfectweddingsbymark.uk is created per test run (intentional — exercises the persistence flow).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 14 spec'd features and 15 backend endpoints verified. data-testids on Dashboard, Auditor, Schema, Locations, Meta, Keywords, Tasks, Compare pages all present. Locations endpoint returns exactly 5 regions / 74 towns. Tasks endpoint returns exactly 50 tasks. Quick audit + Site auditor + compare all hit the real perfectweddingsbymark.uk URL successfully and the score=39 grade=F result matches what the dashboard shows in the screenshot. Bad-URL test confirmed graceful failure with 5xx + toast. No retest needed unless code changes.
